segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: Hey Alina, come on in. Good to finally meet you in person.

segments.speaker: alina-zhou
segments.text: Yeah, you too. This place is really nice, I wasn't expecting it to be so calm in here.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: We try. Okay so I've been looking through your Health Connect data from the last two weeks. Looks like you're pretty active.

segments.speaker: alina-zhou
segments.text: Oh yeah? I try to stay consistent but the last week or so has been kind of a mess honestly.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: We'll get into that. So I saw the football sessions on Saturdays. Is that pickup or more organized?

segments.speaker: alina-zhou
segments.text: Pickup, yeah. Just a group of friends at Dolores Park. It's pretty casual but we do go pretty hard sometimes. Like it's not flag football, there's real contact.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: Right, so cutting, stopping fast, some contact. How did you feel after last Saturday?

segments.speaker: alina-zhou
segments.text: Yeah, my left knee was actually pretty stiff the next morning. Like it wasn't sharp or anything, more like a dull ache when I went down the stairs. It kind of went away by afternoon but it came back Monday.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: Okay, good to know. Has that been a pattern or just last weekend?

segments.speaker: alina-zhou
segments.text: It comes and goes. A few weeks ago it was totally fine. I don't know if it's the turf or just how I plant when I cut.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: We'll keep an eye on it. Okay, the running. Tell me about that.

segments.speaker: alina-zhou
segments.text: I run maybe three or four times a week. Usually three to five miles, sometimes I'll push to six on the weekend. Mostly pavement, I run the Embarcadero a lot.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: And pace, are you tracking that? Easy effort or pushing it?

segments.speaker: alina-zhou
segments.text: Kind of in between. My easy runs are around nine, nine thirty. On a good day I'll push closer to eight. I'm not really training for anything specific.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: Do you notice anything physically on those longer runs? Anything that flares up?

segments.speaker: alina-zhou
segments.text: Yeah, my lower back. It tightens up around mile four or five. Not like stop-running painful, but I definitely notice it. I usually slow down and it settles, but it's pretty consistent.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: Yeah, that's worth paying attention to. What about the gym?

segments.speaker: alina-zhou
segments.text: I lift twice a week. Squats, RDLs, pull-ups, pretty basic. I've been going heavier on squats lately, trying to get my numbers up a little.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: Nice. And how do you feel after those heavy days?

segments.speaker: alina-zhou
segments.text: My neck and traps get really tight. Way more than my legs do, which is kind of annoying because I'm doing squats not shrugs. I think it's how I brace or something.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: That's really common with heavy squats, yeah. We can definitely look at that. What's the work situation like?

segments.speaker: alina-zhou
segments.text: I'm a UX designer so I'm basically on a laptop all day, eight to ten hours. The last week has been brutal, we had a big sprint. My upper back gets tight from sitting, like between the shoulder blades.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: Mm. And sleep? How has that been with everything going on?

segments.speaker: alina-zhou
segments.text: Not great this past week. I fall asleep okay but I wake up a couple times and then I'm groggy in the morning. I think it's just stress, like my brain won't fully turn off.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: Yeah, deadline stress does that. I see it a lot.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: Okay so here's what I'm hearing: left knee stiffness after football, lower back on longer runs, neck and traps after heavy squat days, upper back from the desk hours, and sleep has been off this week.

segments.speaker: alina-zhou
segments.text: Yeah. When you say it all at once it sounds like a lot.

segments.speaker: maya
segments.text: It's a lot of useful information, that's all. That's exactly what we need. Okay, let's get you on the table.
